tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: In Railroad Square, Volunteers Create a Space Where Everyone Belongs
stories.summary: Every Saturday, neighbors gather to share food, toiletries, and supplies—and something more: the simple dignity of seeing each other as human beings.

stories.headline: Rain Itself Becomes Energy: Scientists Power LEDs With Falling Raindrops
stories.summary: Researchers discovered raindrops flowing through pencil-thin tubes generate enough electricity to light 12 LEDs—turning a daily nuisance into clean power.

stories.headline: Minnesota Farmers Get Homegrown Green Fertilizer, Powered by Wind
stories.summary: A new wind-powered ammonia plant gives farmers a clean, local alternative to costly imported fertilizer—grown right where it's needed.

stories.headline: Iron Replaces Platinum: Cheaper, Stronger, Cleaner Fuel Cells Ahead
stories.summary: Researchers swapped expensive platinum for abundant iron in fuel cells without losing performance—a breakthrough that could finally make clean energy affordable.
